---
title: "Desmond Mariita"
subtitle: "AI Assurance & Data Governance"
toc: false
---

I audit AI systems for trustworthiness — fairness, robustness, recourse, and whether their explanations are faithful — and I govern the data behind them. The output is evidence: reproducible tests, a signed self-assessment opinion, and claims that trace to artifacts.

Every writeup here links back to the runnable, tagged repository that produced its numbers. The writeups are the narrative; the repos are the proof.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .hub-col}
### Flagship audits

An end-to-end **self-assessment of a high-risk EU AI Act credit-scoring model** across four properties — recourse, fairness, robustness, and a pre-registered explanation-faithfulness audit — with an Annex-IV-style governance pack, a signed audit opinion, and one-command reproduction. The faithfulness hypotheses were committed to a signed, timestamped record before the model was trained.

[eu-ai-act-credit-assurance](https://github.com/Desmond-Mariita/eu-ai-act-credit-assurance){.hub-link} (v1.1.2, signed)

Five earlier interpretability projects, among them faithfulness benchmarking, modality attribution, calibration trade-offs, and mechanistic probing:

[interpretability-foundations](https://github.com/Desmond-Mariita/interpretability-foundations){.hub-link}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .hub-col}
### Writing

Writeups that live next to the code they describe.

**[When recourse runs backwards](writing/borrow-more/index.qmd)** — the credit model would approve some rejected applicants only if they asked for a *larger* loan. A model-risk case study.

**[Faithfulness is metric-dependent](writing/faithfulness/index.qmd)** — SHAP and LIME on the same model score as faithful, unfaithful, or inconclusive depending on the metric, sign convention, and perturbation regime.

[All writing →](writing/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .hub-col}
### Credentials

MSc Cognitive Systems, University of Potsdam (expected 2026) — thesis: the faithfulness of chain-of-thought explanations in vision–language models. BSc Mathematics & Statistics. Ex-Data Steward at a 150-branch bank.

Certification track in progress: **IAPP AIGP** (sitting ~Sept 2026), **ForHumanity FHCA**, **ISO/IEC 42001 Lead Auditor**, **AWS AI Practitioner**, **IAPP CIPP/E**.

[More about me →](about.qmd)
:::

:::
